title: List Comprehensions
description: Demonstrates Python list comprehensions with a CSV data file.

files.name: main.py
files.content:
# Read scores from data.csv
scores = []
with open('data.csv') as f:
    next(f)  # skip header
    for line in f:
        name, score = line.strip().split(',')
        scores.append((name, int(score)))

# List comprehension: students who passed (score >= 60)
passed = [(name, score) for name, score in scores if score >= 60]
failed = [(name, score) for name, score in scores if score < 60]

print(f"Total students: {len(scores)}")
print(f"Passed ({len(passed)}): {[n for n, _ in passed]}")
print(f"Failed ({len(failed)}): {[n for n, _ in failed]}")

avg = sum(s for _, s in scores) / len(scores)
print(f"Class average: {avg:.1f}")

# Save summary
with open('summary.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write(f'Results summary\n')
    f.write(f'Average score: {avg:.1f}\n')
    for name, score in sorted(scores, key=lambda x: -x[1]):
        status = 'PASS' if score >= 60 else 'FAIL'
        f.write(f'  {name}: {score} [{status}]\n')

print("\nFull summary saved to summary.txt")

files.name: data.csv
files.content:
name,score
Alice,92
Bob,55
Carol,78
Dave,43
Eve,88
Frank,61
Grace,39
Henry,75
